tasks.title: Create planning and repository guardrails for the MVP
tasks.description: Review the current stub repository and establish the planning documents and operating rules that the rest of the implementation will follow.
tasks.details: Audit the existing workspace before implementation. Create `PLANS.md` with milestones, acceptance criteria, verification commands, scope exclusions, and risks. Create `AGENTS.md` with repository-specific operating rules. Create `docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md` as the running log for decisions, progress, and verification results. Keep the current directory structure and package boundaries intact wherever possible.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that `PLANS.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and `docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md` exist and that each document covers the required planning, scope, and verification content from the PRD.

subtasks.title: Audit the current repository structure and implementation gaps
subtasks.description: Inspect the existing workspace to document what is present, what is stubbed, and what is missing before planning begins.
subtasks.details: Review the current directories, package boundaries, and `.taskmaster` materials. Capture the current state of the repo so the planning documents reflect actual starting conditions instead of assumptions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm the audit notes cover the current directories, stubs, and missing implementation areas referenced by the PRD.

subtasks.title: Draft `PLANS.md` with milestone structure and acceptance criteria
subtasks.description: Break the MVP into milestones with clear scope, acceptance conditions, verification commands, and risks.
subtasks.details: Create `PLANS.md` as the implementation plan source of truth. Include milestone goals, target files or directories, acceptance criteria, verification commands, scope exclusions, and risk mitigation tied to the PRD.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify `PLANS.md` includes milestones, verification commands, scope exclusions, and risks that map back to the PRD requirements.

subtasks.title: Write repository operating rules in `AGENTS.md`
subtasks.description: Define the local workflow conventions that implementation work must follow in this repository.
subtasks.details: Document repository-specific rules for milestone-based delivery, verification discipline, scope control, documentation updates, and any local constraints relevant to the MVP implementation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm `AGENTS.md` gives concrete repo rules for implementation, verification, and documentation updates.

subtasks.title: Create `docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md` as the execution log template
subtasks.description: Prepare the document that will hold progress notes, decisions, and verification results throughout implementation.
subtasks.details: Create `docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md` with sections for milestone progress, decisions, verification logs, unresolved limitations, and follow-up notes so later work can append to a stable structure.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the document exists and contains sections for progress, decisions, verification, and limitations.

tasks.title: Stabilize the monorepo and desktop build foundation
tasks.description: Make the pnpm workspace, SvelteKit SPA, Tauri v2 app, and workspace package resolution build cleanly as the implementation base.
tasks.details: Set up or correct the root workspace configuration so `pnpm install` resolves packages. Ensure `apps/desktop` remains a SvelteKit SPA backed by Tauri v2. Fix package manifests, tsconfig setup, path aliases, Vite/Tauri build wiring, and workspace dependency links so the desktop build and Rust check commands can run successfully.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm install`, `pnpm check`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @relay-agent/desktop build`, and `cargo check` successfully from the workspace.

subtasks.title: Establish the root workspace manifests and package links
subtasks.description: Add or fix the root package and workspace configuration so the monorepo resolves correctly.
subtasks.details: Create or update the root `package.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, and related workspace declarations so package discovery and installs work without manual adjustment.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm install` and confirm the workspace resolves all packages without path or manifest errors.

subtasks.title: Align TypeScript config, aliases, and package exports
subtasks.description: Normalize tsconfig layering and package export rules across the workspace.
subtasks.details: Update tsconfig files, path aliases, and package export fields so shared packages such as `packages/contracts` can be consumed cleanly by the desktop app and any future packages.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run workspace typecheck and confirm cross-package imports resolve without alias or export errors.

subtasks.title: Stabilize the SvelteKit, Vite, and Tauri desktop app configuration
subtasks.description: Keep the desktop app as a SvelteKit SPA with Tauri v2 while making dev and build wiring valid.
subtasks.details: Correct `apps/desktop` configuration for SvelteKit SPA mode, Vite integration, and Tauri v2 build expectations so local development and production build paths are consistent.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm --filter @relay-agent/desktop build` successfully.

subtasks.title: Run the baseline verification suite and fix build breakages
subtasks.description: Close the loop on the foundation milestone by executing the required JS and Rust checks.
subtasks.details: Execute the baseline commands from the PRD and address any remaining install, check, typecheck, or cargo issues until the workspace foundation is stable.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm check`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @relay-agent/desktop build`, and `cargo check` without failures.

tasks.title: Normalize the contracts package as the schema source of truth
tasks.description: Define the shared Zod schemas and inferred TypeScript types that all frontend and backend flows will rely on.
tasks.details: Implement and export consistent schemas and inferred types for `Session`, `Turn`, `Item`, `RelayPacket`, `CopilotTurnResponse`, `ToolDescriptor`, `SpreadsheetAction`, `WorkbookProfile`, and `DiffSummary` in `packages/contracts`. Remove broken cross-references and keep `index.ts` as the public export surface used by the desktop app and any generated interfaces.
tasks.testStrategy: Run workspace typecheck and confirm frontend imports compile cleanly against the contracts package without schema/type drift.

subtasks.title: Audit existing `packages/contracts` models and broken references
subtasks.description: Inspect the current contracts package to identify missing exports, drifted types, and invalid cross-schema references.
subtasks.details: Review the current contracts source tree and note where types or schemas are incomplete, duplicated, or out of sync with how the PRD expects them to be used.
subtasks.testStrategy: Produce a complete inventory of contracts that need to be added, merged, or corrected.

subtasks.title: Define the core session, turn, item, and relay schemas
subtasks.description: Implement the foundational schemas for the main relay workflow entities.
subtasks.details: Add or correct the Zod schemas and inferred types for `Session`, `Turn`, `Item`, and `RelayPacket`, keeping relationships explicit and avoiding circular type drift.
subtasks.testStrategy: Typecheck the contracts package and confirm these core workflow entities infer consistent TypeScript types.

subtasks.title: Define Copilot response, tool, workbook, and diff schemas
subtasks.description: Complete the remaining schema layer for model responses and workbook execution artifacts.
subtasks.details: Implement `CopilotTurnResponse`, `ToolDescriptor`, `SpreadsheetAction`, `WorkbookProfile`, and `DiffSummary` so backend parsing and frontend rendering can share one source of truth.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate that all required PRD entities are represented in the contracts package with matching schema and inferred type pairs.

subtasks.title: Export the public package surface and verify consumers compile
subtasks.description: Make `index.ts` the stable export surface for both schemas and types, then verify usage from consumers.
subtasks.details: Re-export the finalized contracts from `index.ts`, remove dead or duplicate exports, and confirm the desktop and backend code can import the package cleanly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run workspace typecheck and confirm no consumer relies on private paths or broken exports.

tasks.title: Implement the Rust agent kernel and typed IPC surface
tasks.description: Build the minimum backend command surface and the matching frontend IPC wrapper for the relay workflow.
tasks.details: Implement the Tauri-side commands for `initialize_app`, `create_session`, `list_sessions`, `read_session`, `start_turn`, `generate_relay_packet`, `submit_copilot_response`, `preview_execution`, `approval/respond`, and `execution/run`. Add a typed IPC wrapper on the frontend that matches the contracts package and keeps request and response payloads consistent.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `cargo check` and frontend typecheck, then verify the desktop app can call each command without request or response type mismatches.

subtasks.title: Scaffold the Rust module layout for app, session, relay, and execution commands
subtasks.description: Create or align the Rust-side module boundaries needed for the MVP command surface.
subtasks.details: Organize the Tauri backend into modules that can hold app initialization, session lifecycle, relay handling, preview generation, approval, and execution behavior without collapsing everything into one file.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `cargo check` to confirm the module skeleton compiles and command registration paths resolve.

subtasks.title: Implement session and turn lifecycle commands
subtasks.description: Add the commands required to initialize the app and manage session and turn state.
subtasks.details: Implement `initialize_app`, `create_session`, `list_sessions`, `read_session`, and `start_turn` using the contracts package payloads and backend storage abstractions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `cargo check` and verify the lifecycle commands can be invoked with the expected request and response shapes.

subtasks.title: Implement relay submission, preview, approval, and execution commands
subtasks.description: Add the backend endpoints needed after a turn starts and the user pastes a model response.
subtasks.details: Implement `generate_relay_packet`, `submit_copilot_response`, `preview_execution`, `approval/respond`, and `execution/run` with consistent command signatures and MVP-safe behavior.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm the backend command set compiles and all command handlers are registered with Tauri.

subtasks.title: Build the typed frontend IPC wrapper for the command surface
subtasks.description: Expose the Rust command set through a typed frontend API layer that uses the shared contracts.
subtasks.details: Implement a typed IPC wrapper in the frontend so the UI can call backend commands using the contracts package instead of ad hoc payload shapes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run frontend typecheck and verify the IPC layer enforces the same request and response types as the backend contracts.

tasks.title: Add local session persistence and artifact storage
tasks.description: Persist sessions, turns, artifacts, and logs under the application data directory so state survives restarts.
tasks.details: Design the app-local storage layout for `sessions`, `turns`, `artifacts`, and `logs`. Serialize session state to local JSON, keep a listable session index, and reload session detail data after app restart. Make persistence conservative and local-only, matching the PRD requirement that sessions remain available across launches.
tasks.testStrategy: Create at least one session, restart the app, and confirm `list_sessions` and `read_session` reload the persisted data from disk.

subtasks.title: Define the application data directory layout and naming conventions
subtasks.description: Design the on-disk structure for sessions, turns, artifacts, and logs.
subtasks.details: Specify where session JSON, turn data, generated artifacts, and logs live under the app-local data directory, including naming and lookup conventions that work across restarts.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review the planned storage layout and confirm it covers sessions, turns, artifacts, and logs without ambiguity.

subtasks.title: Implement session persistence and reload for local JSON storage
subtasks.description: Create the repository logic that writes sessions to disk and reads them back.
subtasks.details: Implement the persistence layer for session creation, update, serialization, and readback using local JSON files in the configured data directory.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create a session, save it, and confirm it can be read back accurately from disk.

subtasks.title: Persist turns, artifacts, and logs with session linkage
subtasks.description: Store turn-level state and related files in a way that preserves session context.
subtasks.details: Write turn records, artifacts, and logs to disk with stable references back to the owning session so preview and execution history remain inspectable.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify a session write also produces recoverable turn, artifact, and log records with correct linkage.

subtasks.title: Verify restart recovery and session list behavior
subtasks.description: Make sure persisted sessions survive relaunch and remain visible in the app.
subtasks.details: Confirm `list_sessions` and `read_session` work after the app restarts and that the session index reflects what was previously written to disk.
subtasks.testStrategy: Restart the app and confirm persisted sessions are listed and readable without manual repair.

tasks.title: Build the frontend MVP studio flow
tasks.description: Implement the minimum user-facing Home, Session Studio, and Settings flows needed to drive the relay workflow end to end.
tasks.details: Create the Home screen with session listing and creation, a Session detail/Studio view with the required three-column layout, and a Settings page with policy placeholders. Add the minimal store-based state management needed for session and turn progression. Wire the UI to the typed IPC layer so users can create sessions, start turns, view relay packets, paste responses, see validation results, and request previews.
tasks.testStrategy: Run the desktop app and manually verify session creation, turn start, relay packet display, pasted response entry, validation result display, and preview button flow.

subtasks.title: Create the app shell and routes for Home, Studio, and Settings
subtasks.description: Set up the minimum frontend route structure required by the MVP.
subtasks.details: Implement the routing and layout shell for the Home page, Session Studio page, and Settings page while preserving the project’s SvelteKit structure.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the desktop app and confirm the three primary routes render without navigation errors.

subtasks.title: Build the Home screen session list and creation flow
subtasks.description: Provide the entry point for creating and selecting sessions.
subtasks.details: Implement the Home page so users can view existing sessions and create a new one using the typed IPC layer and the persisted session store.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify users can create a session from Home and see it appear in the list.

subtasks.title: Build the Studio panes and local state model
subtasks.description: Implement the three-column session detail layout and the minimal store state needed to drive it.
subtasks.details: Create the timeline pane, the center workflow pane for objective, packet, pasted response, validation, and preview controls, and the right pane for workbook summary and diff preview, backed by minimal stores.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the Studio layout renders all required regions and state updates appear in the correct pane.

subtasks.title: Wire the Studio flow to backend commands and validation feedback
subtasks.description: Connect the UI to the relay lifecycle so the flow is usable end to end.
subtasks.details: Use the IPC wrapper to drive session selection, turn start, relay packet generation, pasted response submission, validation result display, and preview requests from the Studio screen.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manually verify session creation, turn start, packet display, pasted response handling, validation feedback, and preview initiation.

tasks.title: Implement relay packet generation and Copilot response validation
tasks.description: Generate the relay prompts and validate pasted Copilot responses into structured actions the app can preview and execute.
tasks.details: Implement `packet_builder.rs` to generate `discover`, `plan`, `repair`, and `followup` relay packets. Supply `allowedReadTools` and `allowedWriteTools` from a fixed registry. Implement `response_parser.rs` to parse pasted JSON, validate it against contracts, and return structured validation errors. Generate repair-oriented follow-up packet text when pasted responses fail validation.
tasks.testStrategy: Feed both valid and invalid response samples through submission and verify structured parsing, validation error reporting, and repair follow-up generation.

subtasks.title: Define the fixed read and write tool registry
subtasks.description: Lock down the allowed tool surface that relay packets and response validation will reference.
subtasks.details: Create the canonical registry for allowed read and write tools so relay packet generation and response validation use the same fixed source instead of dynamic inference.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm the registry covers the MVP tool set and can be referenced from both packet building and validation code.

subtasks.title: Implement `packet_builder.rs` for discover, plan, repair, and followup modes
subtasks.description: Generate the user-facing relay packets for each stage of the Copilot handoff flow.
subtasks.details: Implement relay packet construction using the fixed tool registry and session context so the app can generate discover, plan, repair, and follow-up prompts consistently.
subtasks.testStrategy: Generate sample packets for each mode and verify the correct tool allowances and context appear.

subtasks.title: Implement `response_parser.rs` for pasted JSON parsing and validation
subtasks.description: Parse user-pasted Copilot output into contract-backed structures and return useful errors when parsing fails.
subtasks.details: Parse pasted JSON into `CopilotTurnResponse`, validate it against the shared contracts, and return structured validation errors rather than opaque failures.
subtasks.testStrategy: Submit valid and invalid sample payloads and confirm parsing succeeds or returns structured validation errors accordingly.

subtasks.title: Generate repair follow-ups and validation fixtures
subtasks.description: Close the validation loop by producing repair prompts and a stable sample set for regression checks.
subtasks.details: Generate repair-oriented follow-up text when validation fails and add sample valid and invalid payload fixtures to support future regression checks.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify failed submissions return actionable repair follow-ups and that fixtures exercise both success and failure paths.

tasks.title: Implement the workbook inspection and CSV-first transformation engine
tasks.description: Deliver the safe vertical slice for workbook inspection, profiling, and CSV-based transformations, with xlsx support limited to inspect and save-copy priorities.
tasks.details: Prioritize a CSV end-to-end flow that is safe and demonstrable. Implement the read tools `workbook.inspect`, `sheet.preview`, `sheet.profile_columns`, and `session.diff_from_base`. Implement the minimum write tools `table.rename_columns`, `table.cast_columns`, `table.filter_rows`, `table.derive_column`, `table.group_aggregate`, and `workbook.save_copy`. Treat xlsx fidelity work as secondary, focusing first on inspect and save-copy. Leave `join_lookup` as a stub with TODOs if it is not feasible inside the MVP scope.
tasks.testStrategy: Use a small CSV sample to inspect workbook state and generate previews for each supported action without mutating the original source file.

subtasks.title: Select workbook libraries and define engine module boundaries
subtasks.description: Choose the CSV and xlsx handling approach that keeps the MVP safe and maintainable.
subtasks.details: Pick stable libraries for CSV and limited xlsx support, then define the engine modules so inspect, preview, and write-preview logic can be implemented cleanly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review the selected library approach and confirm it supports CSV end-to-end plus limited xlsx inspect and save-copy behavior.

subtasks.title: Implement the read-side workbook tools
subtasks.description: Add the read-only tools required for workbook inspection and diff comparison.
subtasks.details: Implement `workbook.inspect`, `sheet.preview`, `sheet.profile_columns`, and `session.diff_from_base` using the workbook engine boundaries chosen for the MVP.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the read tools against a sample file and confirm inspection, preview, profiling, and diff data return correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement the core CSV-first write-preview tools
subtasks.description: Support the minimum transformation set needed for the MVP execution preview flow.
subtasks.details: Implement `table.rename_columns`, `table.cast_columns`, `table.filter_rows`, and `table.derive_column` with CSV safety and previewability in mind.
subtasks.testStrategy: Generate previews for each core transformation on sample CSV input and verify the source file is not mutated.

subtasks.title: Add aggregation, save-copy support, and CSV demo verification
subtasks.description: Complete the remaining MVP workbook operations and validate the vertical slice on sample data.
subtasks.details: Implement `table.group_aggregate` and `workbook.save_copy`, and leave `join_lookup` as a documented stub if necessary. Verify the CSV path supports end-to-end inspection and preview behavior on sample data.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the CSV demo flow through inspect and preview paths and confirm save-copy behavior is available without touching the original file.

tasks.title: Add execution preview, approval flow, and safe save-copy execution
tasks.description: Show a diff summary before writes, require approval, and run changes through save-copy only behavior with the specified safety constraints.
tasks.details: Implement `preview_execution` so it returns at least the target sheet, added or changed columns, estimated affected row count, and output destination. Surface that preview clearly in the UI and add an approval path before any write. Implement `execution/run` so it writes only to a copy, keeps the original file read-only, sanitizes CSV output against formula injection, avoids arbitrary code execution, shell access, VBA, network access, and rejects raw Excel formulas from model output.
tasks.testStrategy: Complete a session-to-preview-to-approval-to-run flow on a CSV sample and verify the original file remains unchanged, the output is written to a copy, and dangerous CSV cell prefixes are sanitized.

subtasks.title: Define the preview payload and diff summary structure
subtasks.description: Formalize the data returned by `preview_execution` before any writes are approved.
subtasks.details: Specify the preview payload so it includes the target sheet, changed or added columns, approximate affected row count, and output destination in a stable `DiffSummary`-aligned shape.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm the preview payload shape supports every UI field required by the PRD.

subtasks.title: Implement backend preview generation from parsed actions
subtasks.description: Turn validated action plans into a concrete pre-execution summary on the backend.
subtasks.details: Generate execution previews from parsed spreadsheet actions and workbook context so the backend can explain what will happen before any file output is written.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify `preview_execution` returns meaningful summaries for supported CSV actions.

subtasks.title: Render diff preview and approval flow in the Studio UI
subtasks.description: Expose the preview result and explicit approval step to the user before execution.
subtasks.details: Show the execution preview in the right-side diff area and add an approval path that must be completed before a write-capable execution can proceed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the UI shows preview details clearly and prevents execution until approval is granted.

subtasks.title: Enforce save-copy only execution safeguards and CSV sanitization
subtasks.description: Apply the safety rules that keep the MVP within the allowed execution envelope.
subtasks.details: Ensure execution writes only to a copy, treats the original workbook as read-only, sanitizes CSV cells starting with `=`, `+`, `-`, or `@`, and rejects unsafe inputs such as shell, VBA, arbitrary code, network access, or raw Excel formulas from model output.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run a CSV execution flow and confirm output is save-copy only, the original file stays unchanged, and unsafe CSV prefixes are neutralized.

tasks.title: Ship demo assets, README guidance, and implementation log updates
tasks.description: Add the examples and documentation needed to run, understand, and evaluate the MVP consistently.
tasks.details: Add `examples/` with at least one small CSV sample. Update `README` with startup instructions, the demo flow, a sample relay packet, a minimal valid pasted Copilot response, and known limitations. Keep `docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md` updated with milestone decisions, progress notes, and verification results so later work can follow the documented source of truth.
tasks.testStrategy: Follow the documented README flow on a clean workspace and confirm the CSV demo works as described and the documented limitations match the implementation state.

subtasks.title: Add example CSV assets for the MVP demo flow
subtasks.description: Provide a small sample dataset that exercises the inspect, preview, and save-copy path.
subtasks.details: Create `examples/` and add at least one compact CSV sample suited to the demo transformations and preview flow described in the PRD.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the example data can be loaded and used in the MVP workflow without additional manual setup.

subtasks.title: Update `README` with setup, packet, and response examples
subtasks.description: Document how to start the app and what relay input and output should look like.
subtasks.details: Add startup instructions, the demo flow, a sample relay packet to paste into Copilot, and a minimal valid Copilot JSON response example that matches the implemented contracts.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review the README and confirm a new contributor can reach the demo flow using only documented steps.

subtasks.title: Keep `docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md` aligned with milestones and verification results
subtasks.description: Record the actual implementation progress and verification outcomes as work lands.
subtasks.details: Update `docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md` with milestone decisions, execution notes, command results, and known limitations so it reflects the real implementation instead of an aspirational plan.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm the implementation log tracks milestone progress, verification output, and known limitations in a current state.

subtasks.title: Run the documented demo flow and reconcile docs with reality
subtasks.description: Use the example assets and docs to verify the MVP can be demonstrated as described.
subtasks.details: Follow the README steps against the example CSV flow, compare the actual app behavior to the docs, and tighten any mismatches or missing limitation notes before considering the MVP documented.
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute the documented demo path and verify the README and known limitations match actual behavior.

tasks.title: Package the app and simplify startup for non-engineer operators
tasks.description: Turn startup into a packaged-app-first flow with first-run guidance, permission explanations, recovery actions, and diagnostic export.
tasks.details: Implement the follow-up PRD's launch and first-run scope: supported OS and installer decisions, packaged launch path, `initialize_app` startup preflight messaging, friendly failure states, permission rationale, self-recovery actions, and diagnostic export. Preserve the current backend safety model and keep developer-oriented CLI startup outside the end-user path.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify the follow-up implementation provides a packaged-app-first startup path, first-run welcome states, permission explanations, recoverable startup errors, and diagnostic export behavior without requiring terminal commands.

subtasks.title: Define packaging, installer, and update policy for the first supported OS
subtasks.description: Choose and document the official end-user distribution target and update expectations.
subtasks.details: Set the initial supported OS, bundle target, installer type, update model, and data retention expectations so the app has one official non-engineer launch story instead of a development-only startup path.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm the supported-platform, install, update, and data-retention rules are reflected in planning docs and packaging configuration expectations.

subtasks.title: Implement startup preflight, friendly failure states, and self-recovery
subtasks.description: Make initialization and storage issues understandable and recoverable from the app UI.
subtasks.details: Extend the startup flow so missing app data storage, inaccessible save locations, or first-run setup problems show plain-language guidance, retry, reset, and support-oriented diagnostic actions instead of technical errors.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify startup failures surface clear problem, reason, and next-step guidance plus at least one retry, reset, or diagnostic action.

subtasks.title: Build first-run welcome, sample/custom entry, and permission rationale
subtasks.description: Guide users into the app with a safe first-run choice and pre-permission explanations.
subtasks.details: Add a first-run welcome surface that explains what the app does, highlights save-copy behavior, offers sample or real-file entry points, and explains OS permission prompts before the system dialog appears.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify first-run users can choose sample or real-file entry and understand why any permission prompt is needed before the OS dialog opens.

subtasks.title: Align packaged-startup docs and verification with the implemented flow
subtasks.description: Keep the planning and execution logs in sync once the startup UX is real.
subtasks.details: Update `PLANS.md`, `docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md`, and any real user-facing setup docs so the packaged-app-first startup flow, recovery actions, and diagnostic export behavior stay documented and verifiable.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm the implementation log and real setup docs describe the same startup flow and verification steps that the app actually supports.

tasks.title: Add data trust, file readiness, and safe handoff guidance
tasks.description: Make local storage, file compatibility, and Copilot handoff safety understandable before work starts.
tasks.details: Implement the PRD scope around local-only data handling explanations, file preflight checks, early constraint surfacing, locale and CSV compatibility guidance, and pre-copy sensitivity warnings for confidential or personal data before users hand anything to Copilot.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify users are told what stays local, what is not auto-sent externally, whether a file is safe to use, and when a Copilot handoff may expose sensitive data.

subtasks.title: Explain local-only storage, retention, and deletion behavior
subtasks.description: Surface what the app stores locally and how users can remove it.
subtasks.details: Define and expose clear copy for local session storage, logs, generated file references, retention expectations, and deletion paths so non-engineers can understand the trust model without reading technical docs.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify Home or Studio surfaces what data is kept locally, what is not auto-sent, and how users can delete saved app data.

subtasks.title: Add file preflight, constraint surfacing, and locale or CSV compatibility guidance
subtasks.description: Warn users early about unreadable files, unsupported inputs, or format mismatches.
subtasks.details: Implement preflight checks for file readability, lock state, size hints, unsupported workbook complexity, and locale-driven CSV issues such as encoding, delimiters, date formats, decimal separators, and thousands separators.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify users see plain-language preflight results and early constraint messages before starting a flow with unsupported or risky inputs.

subtasks.title: Warn before Copilot copy when confidential or personal data may be included
subtasks.description: Insert a lightweight sensitivity check before users copy a relay packet out of the app.
subtasks.details: Add a pre-copy caution that highlights possible personal data, customer identifiers, or confidential fields and reminds users to share only the minimum necessary content before continuing to Copilot.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the app shows a short caution before copy when file or column context suggests confidential or personal data may be present.

subtasks.title: Verify risky inputs and handoffs are blocked or clarified before execution
subtasks.description: Close the loop on file-readiness and trust checks before the app reaches preview or save.
subtasks.details: Add verification coverage that proves unsupported files, locale or CSV mismatches, and sensitive-data copy warnings are surfaced before preview or execution instead of appearing as late-stage failures.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run targeted UI and backend verification for preflight messaging, locale or CSV warnings, and copy-time sensitivity cautions.

tasks.title: Implement resumable work, crash recovery, and leave-safe continuity
tasks.description: Keep in-progress work recoverable across restart, abnormal shutdown, and intentional exit.
tasks.details: Implement the PRD continuity scope: resumable drafts, recent items, abnormal-shutdown recovery, unsaved or unreviewed leave warnings, and clearer retry, discard, or keep-draft choices so non-engineers do not lose context mid-flow.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify in-progress work can be resumed after restart, restored after abnormal shutdown, and safely left with clear warnings and recovery choices.

subtasks.title: Persist resumable drafts, preview state, and recent items
subtasks.description: Save enough local state for users to continue where they left off.
subtasks.details: Persist objective drafts, pasted response drafts, preview state, output-path candidates, recent sessions, and recent files so users can re-enter the flow without redoing setup work.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify restart shows recent work and restores draft plus preview context for resumable sessions.

subtasks.title: Add crash recovery from autosaved state after abnormal shutdown
subtasks.description: Offer a safe restore flow after the app or machine exits unexpectedly.
subtasks.details: Store a minimal safe autosave state and present a recovery prompt on the next launch so users can restore or discard the previous in-progress work after a crash or reboot.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify an abnormal shutdown leads to a recovery prompt and that restored state matches the last safe autosave instead of a partially corrupted draft.

subtasks.title: Add unsaved or unreviewed leave warnings and clear retry or discard choices
subtasks.description: Prevent users from accidentally losing work when closing or backing out.
subtasks.details: Warn before leaving dirty drafts or unreviewed preview states, and make the difference between retry, keep draft, discard, and close explicit in plain language.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify closing or backing out with dirty state shows clear leave options and preserves or discards work exactly as labeled.

subtasks.title: Verify continuity flows across restart, crash recovery, and intentional exit

subtasks.details: Add verification for restart-based resume, abnormal-shutdown restore, recent-item recall, and leave-warning actions so continuity behavior stays predictable as the follow-up UX evolves.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run continuity-focused tests and manual walkthroughs for resume, restore, discard, and keep-draft behavior.

tasks.title: Guide non-engineer users with templates, onboarding, and inline help
tasks.description: Reduce first-use friction with sample-first entry, safe defaults, templates, and contextual guidance.
tasks.details: Implement the guided-start portion of the PRD: sample-first onboarding, plain-language objective entry, template-driven starts, safe defaults, and contextual inline help that lets users complete the flow without understanding technical vocabulary or reading the README first.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify a first-time user can start from the sample flow or a real file, understand the next step, and reach the review stage using only in-product guidance.

subtasks.title: Add sample-first onboarding and plain-language objective entry
subtasks.description: Lead first-time users into a successful start without technical wording.
subtasks.details: Prioritize a bundled sample path on first run, simplify the objective prompt into business-language examples, and keep the initial path focused on one clear next action at a time.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify first-time users can choose a sample path and understand how to describe their goal without using internal workflow terms.

subtasks.title: Add template-driven starts and safe defaults
subtasks.description: Provide common starting points and hide advanced configuration by default.
subtasks.details: Offer common spreadsheet tasks such as rename, cast, filter, derive, and aggregate as template starts while keeping save-copy, review, and output-path behaviors on safe defaults unless the user explicitly changes them.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify common tasks can start from templates and that first-run flows do not require opening advanced settings.

subtasks.title: Add inline help and glossary that matches the current step
subtasks.description: Let users resolve uncertainty inside the UI instead of leaving for documentation.
subtasks.details: Show short first-run hints by default and keep deeper help behind an explicit affordance so users can re-check terms and next actions without cluttering the main flow.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify users can re-open concise help for the current step and that the UI does not expose unnecessary technical detail by default.

subtasks.title: Verify the guided flow works without README dependency
subtasks.description: Confirm the in-product guidance is sufficient for first-use success.
subtasks.details: Run guided-flow verification to ensure first-time users can move from onboarding through request entry and Copilot handoff using the app's own prompts, templates, and help surfaces.
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute a first-run walkthrough without referring to README and confirm the user can reach the review step using only the implemented UX cues.

tasks.title: Simplify review, save, reviewer confirmation, and operational history
tasks.description: Turn the current technical execution path into a clearer review-and-save flow with reviewer-safe surfaces and local operation history.
tasks.details: Implement the PRD's execution-phase simplification: one review-and-save UX over the existing preview and approval gates, three-point summaries, output-path suggestion, filename collision guards, progress visibility, duplicate-run prevention, completion actions, reviewer-friendly summaries, read-only review mode, and local audit history.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify users can review, save, and share outcomes through one clear flow while the backend approval and save-copy guardrails remain intact.

subtasks.title: Collapse preview, approval, and execution into a review-and-save UX
subtasks.description: Hide backend stage jargon behind a simpler user-facing step model.
subtasks.details: Rework Studio so users see a plain-language stepper and a single review-and-save phase while the existing preview and approval requirements remain enforced behind the scenes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify users can complete the save flow without understanding preview or approval terminology and that approval is still required before any write-capable execution.

subtasks.title: Add three-point review summary, progress visibility, and output-path safety
subtasks.description: Keep the main review information short, visible, and safe.
subtasks.details: Show what will change, how many rows are affected, and where output goes at the top of the review step, while also surfacing progress states, output-path suggestions, and filename collision warnings in plain language.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify review screens always surface the three key summary points plus progress and filename safeguards before save actions are enabled.

subtasks.title: Add duplicate-run prevention, completion actions, reviewer summaries, and read-only review mode
subtasks.description: Prevent accidental re-runs and support fast review by non-executing stakeholders.
subtasks.details: Disable duplicate save actions while work is in progress, add clear post-run next steps, generate reviewer-friendly summaries, and provide a read-only review surface that exposes changes without execution controls.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify repeated save clicks do not create duplicate work and that reviewer-only users can inspect change summaries without execution access.

subtasks.title: Add local audit history for inputs, outputs, timestamps, and change summaries
subtasks.description: Keep a human-readable record of what work was performed and when.
subtasks.details: Persist and surface a local operation history that records the input file, output file, execution time, and summary of changes so support and approval follow-up do not depend on memory or manual notes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify completed runs appear in local operation history with the expected input, output, timestamp, and change-summary fields.

tasks.title: Harden non-technical recovery, trust communication, and accessibility
tasks.description: Make errors repairable, safety guarantees visible, and the follow-up UX accessible enough for everyday use.
tasks.details: Implement the remaining cross-cutting PRD work: non-technical validation and execution errors, Copilot re-prompt support, always-visible original-file and save-copy guidance, accessibility baselines, and the final documentation plus verification pass for the non-engineer follow-up milestone set.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify errors always explain the next recovery action, trust messaging stays visible in key steps, accessibility basics are covered, and the final follow-up docs match the real behavior.

subtasks.title: Rewrite validation and execution failures into non-technical copy with re-prompt support

subtasks.testStrategy: Verify failed responses and execution problems show at least one concrete correction path plus a reusable Copilot follow-up prompt when applicable.

subtasks.title: Keep original-file protection and save-copy-only guidance visible across the app
subtasks.description: Reinforce the safety model in user-facing language at the points where confidence matters most.
subtasks.details: Surface steady trust messaging in Home, Studio, review, and completion states so users can always see that the original file remains read-only and writes only go to a save-copy output.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the app consistently shows original-file and save-copy protections in key entry, review, and completion states.

subtasks.title: Apply the accessibility baseline for text, contrast, keyboard flow, and status cues
subtasks.description: Meet the minimum UX accessibility bar defined by the follow-up PRD.
subtasks.details: Audit and improve typography, contrast, focus order, keyboard completion paths, and non-color state indicators across the non-engineer follow-up surfaces so the UX does not depend on perfect vision or mouse-only operation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the main flow is keyboard-navigable, readable at the chosen default text sizes, and understandable without relying on color alone.

subtasks.title: Run the follow-up verification pass and align planning or implementation docs with reality
subtasks.description: Close the loop on the non-engineer milestone set with documented evidence.
subtasks.details: Execute the agreed verification commands, reconcile `PLANS.md`, `docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md`, and any real user docs with the shipped follow-up behavior, and record remaining limitations instead of leaving the scope aspirational.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm verification artifacts exist, docs match the implementation, and unresolved follow-up limitations are recorded explicitly.

tasks.title: Render column profiling and diff artifacts in both editable and reviewer Studio

tasks.testStrategy: Verify reviewer mode can inspect the same artifact browser without execution controls and that column-profile plus diff artifacts render correctly.

tasks.title: Document and verify the workbook artifact browser follow-up
tasks.description: Close the new follow-up scope with synced docs, Task Master state, and a manual verification artifact.
tasks.details: Update planning and implementation docs, add a manual verification checklist for the artifact browser, and sync README plus Task Master so the shipped scope is documented as real behavior rather than aspirational follow-up.
tasks.testStrategy: Confirm the verification artifact exists, docs match the implementation, Task Master reflects the completed work, and the standard repo checks still pass.

tasks.title: Define turn inspection artifact contracts for lifecycle details
tasks.description: Add summary-first contracts for packet, validation, approval, execution, and turn-overview inspection records.
tasks.details: Define lifecycle inspection artifact schemas covering at least `packet-summary`, `validation-summary`, `approval-record`, `execution-summary`, and `turn-overview`. Include shared metadata such as `sessionId`, `turnId`, `mode`, `status`, `createdAt`, `schemaVersion`, and `sourceType` so live and persisted sources can be normalized later.
tasks.testStrategy: Add schema validation tests, fixture-based compatibility tests, and legacy fallback coverage for older turns that do not yet have lifecycle inspection artifacts.

tasks.title: Emit turn lifecycle summary artifacts from backend commands
tasks.description: Generate lifecycle inspection summaries when packet, validation, approval, and execution state changes.
tasks.details: When relay packet generation, validation, approval, or execution runs, emit the new summary artifacts and persist the supported records through the existing artifact storage path. For current turns, keep equivalent runtime summaries available even before persistence exists so temporary mode and in-progress turns can still render inspection details.
tasks.testStrategy: Use integration tests to cover happy path, validation failure, execution failure, and temporary-mode behavior, and verify the expected lifecycle summary artifacts or runtime summaries are available at each step.

tasks.title: Build a TurnDetails resolver across live and persisted state
tasks.description: Unify runtime and stored lifecycle evidence behind one UI-facing view model.
tasks.details: Create a `TurnDetailsViewModel` resolver that merges live runtime state and persisted artifacts into one read-only inspection payload for the UI. The resolver should centralize source priority, missing-step reasoning, temporary-mode messaging, and legacy-turn fallbacks so the frontend does not branch on storage internals.
tasks.testStrategy: Add unit coverage for live-only, persisted-only, both-available, legacy turn, failed-before-step, and expired-temporary-state scenarios.

tasks.title: Add the Turn Details shell and overview to Inspection details
tasks.description: Extend Studio with a read-only lifecycle inspection shell without changing the existing write flow.
tasks.details: Extend the existing `Inspection details` information architecture with a `Turn details` entry point, lifecycle overview, status badges, mode badges, and explicit unavailable states for current turns, persisted turns, temporary mode, and unsupported legacy turns.
tasks.testStrategy: Cover the shell with component or route tests, accessibility checks, and fixtures for current-turn, persisted-turn, temporary-mode, and unavailable-state rendering.

tasks.title: Render packet, validation, approval, and execution summaries
tasks.description: Implement summary-first read-only renderers for each lifecycle category and their cross-references.
tasks.details: Implement category-specific read-only renderers for packet, validation, approval, and execution, using plain-language summaries first and only limited detail disclosure afterward. Include links back to related preview or diff evidence where relevant, but do not add any new preview, approval, execution, or retry actions from this surface.
tasks.testStrategy: Use renderer tests and flow-level UI checks for success, warning, and failure cases to confirm summaries, status labels, and cross-references render correctly.

tasks.title: Integrate turn lifecycle details with reviewer mode, history, and docs

tasks.testStrategy: Run reviewer-mode flow tests, history-entry regression checks, documentation checklist review, and a manual smoke test that confirms the new lifecycle inspection surface stays read-only across modes.

Cover ready state, retry-based recovery, sample workbook discovery, and attention-state startup issues without changing product guardrails.

tasks.title: Abolish sample/custom entry mode split on Home
tasks.description: Remove the entryMode concept and the 'Try sample' / 'Use my own file' fork from the Home page, replacing them with a single unified form.
tasks.details: Delete `entryMode`, `startSampleFlow()`, `startCustomFlow()`, `showGuidedStartGate`, and the conditional rendering that depends on them in `+page.svelte` (Home). Replace the guided-start gate with a single always-visible form containing: file selector, objective template chips, and task-name field. Merge `sampleObjectiveStarters`, `customObjectiveStarters`, `sampleQuickStartTemplates`, and `customQuickStartTemplates` into one unified template list. Bundled CSV becomes a selectable template option in the file selector, not a separate flow. The `始める` button is the single primary CTA. PRD ref: FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-9.
tasks.testStrategy: Home page loads with no sample/custom fork. Bundled CSV appears as a selectable template. Objective starters are a single merged list. The guided-start gate no longer appears.

tasks.title: Auto-generate task name from objective and wire Home→Studio handoff

On `始める`, perform `createSession` + `startTurn` in one aggregated call so Studio receives a session with an active turn and can proceed to packet generation immediately. Pass title, objective, relayMode, and workbookPath through the route query or a shared store. PRD ref: FR-2.4, FR-2.5.
tasks.testStrategy: Selecting an objective template populates the title field. Title is editable. Pressing 始める creates a session AND a turn, navigates to Studio where the turn is already active.

tasks.title: Delete demo response code and Load demo response button
tasks.description: Remove all demo-response machinery from the codebase: buildDemoResponse, loadDemoResponse, demoResponseAvailable, and the Load demo response button in Studio.
tasks.details: Delete `buildDemoResponse()`, `loadDemoResponse()`, `buildDemoOutputPath()`, and the `demoResponseAvailable` reactive binding from `studio/+page.svelte`. Remove the `Load demo response` chip-button from the pasted-response subpanel. Remove any conditional logic or help text that references demo responses. This is a pure removal task with no replacement feature. PRD ref: FR-9.
tasks.testStrategy: Grep for 'demo', 'demoResponse', 'loadDemoResponse', 'buildDemoResponse', 'buildDemoOutputPath' in the frontend source — zero hits. Studio compiles and renders without errors.

tasks.title: Implement GuidedStage state machine and step banner component
tasks.description: Add a GuidedStage type (setup | copilot | review-save) with transition logic, and render a 3-step progress banner at the top of Studio.
tasks.details: Create a `GuidedStage` type with three values: `setup`, `copilot`, `review-save`. Each stage has a `completed | current | waiting` display state derived from turn status and command results. Build a `StepBanner.svelte` component that renders: ① はじめる — ② Copilot に聞く — ③ 確認して保存, with the current stage highlighted and a one-line description beneath it. The banner replaces or overlays the existing `stageTimeline` rendering in guided mode. PRD ref: FR-1.1.
tasks.testStrategy: Banner renders 3 steps. Stage transitions correctly as turn progresses through draft → packet-ready → validated → preview-ready → approved → executed.

tasks.title: Implement single primary button per stage
tasks.description: Replace the multi-button action-row with a single primary button that changes label and handler based on the current GuidedStage.
tasks.details: In guided mode, hide the current action-row (Start turn / Generate packet / Validate response / Check changes / Reset draft). Instead show one `PrimaryAction.svelte` component. Stage mapping: setup → 'Copilot への依頼を準備する' (calls generateRelayPacket), copilot → '変更を確認する' (calls auto-fix + submitCopilotResponse + previewExecution), review-save → 'コピーを保存する' (calls respondToApproval + runExecution). Below the button, always show a one-line explanation of what will happen. The setup stage aggregates startTurn + generateRelayPacket if the turn has not started yet. PRD ref: FR-1.2.
tasks.testStrategy: Only one primary button is visible at a time. Label changes per stage. Each button triggers the correct aggregated backend calls. The one-line explanation is always visible.

tasks.title: Add multi-command progress indicator for aggregated actions
tasks.description: Show a step-by-step progress checklist when a single button triggers multiple backend commands in sequence.
tasks.details: When the primary button fires an aggregated action (e.g. setup: createSession → startTurn → generatePacket), display a progress list with checkmarks. Each step shows ✓ on success, ✗ on failure with a plain-language message. On failure, show a [やり直す] button and hide internal command names. The progress replaces the button while running and yields back to the next stage on success. PRD ref: FR-1.3.
tasks.testStrategy: During aggregated setup action, progress steps appear in order. On intentional failure (e.g. bad path), error message is non-technical and retry button works.

tasks.title: Add expert-details toggle and hide advanced controls in guided mode
tasks.description: Wrap the existing inspection details, artifact browser, turn-details tabs, and multi-button action-row in a collapsible 'Expert details' toggle that is closed by default.
tasks.details: Add a disclosure widget labeled '詳細表示' to Studio. When closed (default), the inspection-details panel, artifact browser, turn-details tabs, and the raw action-row are hidden. When open, all current controls render as before. The toggle state persists per session via continuity. PRD ref: FR-8.
tasks.testStrategy: Expert details toggle is present and closed by default. Opening it reveals all inspection surfaces. Closing hides them. Toggle state persists across page reloads.

tasks.title: Redesign Copilot instruction text as natural-language + template format
tasks.description: Replace the raw relay-packet JSON copy with a human-readable instruction text that includes objective, allowed tools, response format rules, JSON template, and a concrete example.
tasks.details: After generateRelayPacket, build a Copilot-facing instruction string (not the raw JSON packet). Structure: 1) natural-language objective, 2) target file + sheet, 3) allowed tools as bullet list, 4) strict format rules ('no markdown fence', 'use / not \\ for paths', 'JSON only'), 5) JSON template with placeholders, 6) a concrete response example derived from the workbook profile when available. The 'Copilot 用にコピー' button copies this text instead of the raw packet. The raw packet is preserved in expert details. PRD ref: FR-3.1.
tasks.testStrategy: Copied text contains natural-language objective, tool list, 'do not wrap in ```' instruction, JSON template, and an example. Raw packet is still accessible in expert details.

tasks.title: Implement JSON auto-fix pipeline before validation
tasks.description: Add a preprocessing step that automatically repairs common Copilot response issues (markdown fences, trailing commas, BOM, CRLF, Windows paths) before submitting to validation.
tasks.details: Create a `autoFixCopilotResponse(raw: string): { fixed: string; fixes: string[] }` function. Apply fixes in order: 1) strip markdown fences (```json...```), 2) trim whitespace, 3) remove BOM (U+FEFF), 4) normalize CRLF→LF, 5) remove trailing commas in arrays/objects, 6) replace \\ with / in JSON string values only. Return the cleaned string and a list of human-readable fix descriptions. Call this before `submitCopilotResponse`. PRD ref: FR-4.1.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: fence-wrapped JSON → clean. Trailing comma → clean. BOM → clean. CRLF → LF. Windows paths → forward slashes. Each fix produces the correct description string. Combined input with multiple issues → all fixed.

tasks.title: scripts/copilot-browser.ts — CDP 接続・ページナビゲーション
tasks.description: scripts/copilot-browser.ts に Edge CDP 接続・M365 Copilot Chat へのナビゲーション・接続確認を実装する。Node.js スクリプトとして `node scripts/copilot-browser.js --action connect` で実行可能にする。
tasks.details: 実装内容: (1) `chromium.connectOverCDP('http://localhost:{cdpPort}')` で接続。CDP 接続失敗時は { status: 'error', errorCode: 'CDP_UNAVAILABLE', message: 'Edge が CDP モードで起動していません。' } を stdout に JSON で出力して終了。(2) Copilot Chat URL でなければ `page.goto(COPILOT_URL)` でナビゲート。ログインページ検知（URL パターンマッチ）時は { status: 'error', errorCode: 'NOT_LOGGED_IN' } を返す。(3) ページ安定確認後 { status: 'ready' } を stdout に出力。(4) CLI 引数: `--cdp-port` (default: 9222) / `--timeout` (default: 30000ms)。esbuild で `scripts/dist/copilot-browser.js` にバンドル。package.json の scripts に `"copilot-browser:build": "esbuild scripts/copilot-browser.ts --bundle --platform=node --outfile=scripts/dist/copilot-browser.js"` を追加。
tasks.testStrategy: Edge を CDP モードで起動した状態で `node scripts/dist/copilot-browser.js --action connect` を実行し、stdout に { status: 'ready' } が出力されることを手動確認。Edge 未起動の状態で { errorCode: 'CDP_UNAVAILABLE' } が返ることを確認。

tasks.title: scripts/copilot-browser.ts — プロンプト送信・レスポンスキャプチャ
tasks.description: copilot-browser.ts に `--action send` モードを実装する。新規チャット開始・プロンプト入力・送信・`waitForResponse()` による API 応答キャプチャ・テキスト抽出を行い、stdout に JSON で返す。
tasks.details: 実装内容: (1) `--action send` 時は stdin から JSON { prompt: string } を読み取る。(2) `getByTestId('newChatButton').click()` で新規チャット開始（前の文脈をリセット）。(3) プロンプト入力: `page.getByRole('textbox').fill(prompt)` または Task 76 で収集した確認済みセレクタを使用。(4) 送信と同時に `page.waitForResponse(r => r.url().includes(API_ENDPOINT_PATTERN))` を設定して API 応答をキャプチャ。SSE/NDJSON の場合は最後の有効チャンクからテキストを抽出。(5) API キャプチャが失敗した場合の DOM フォールバック: 応答エリアテキストをポーリングで取得。(6) 引用ラベル（[1] 等）を stripCitations() で除去。(7) Copilot エラー応答パターンを検知して最大2回リトライ。(8) stdout に { status: 'ok', response: string } または { status: 'error', errorCode: string, message: string } を出力。
tasks.testStrategy: 実際の M365 Copilot に短いテストプロンプト（「こんにちは、テストです」）を送信し、stdout の response フィールドにテキストが含まれることを手動確認。エラー応答のシミュレーションでリトライが動作することを確認。

tasks.title: Tauri ↔ Node.js 連携（IPC コマンドと shell plugin 設定）
tasks.description: Tauri の @tauri-apps/plugin-shell を使って scripts/dist/copilot-browser.js を呼び出す IPC コマンドを実装する。Rust コマンドは不要で、フロントエンドから直接 Command.create() で呼び出す。
tasks.details: 実装内容: (1) tauri.conf.json の capabilities に node コマンドの実行許可を追加: { identifier: 'shell:allow-execute', allow: [{ name: 'node', cmd: 'node', sidecar: false, args: true }] }。(2) packages/contracts/src/ipc.ts に CopilotBrowserRequest / CopilotBrowserResponse Zod スキーマを追加。(3) lib/copilot-browser.ts を新規作成: sendToCopilot(prompt: string): Promise<string> を実装。内部で Command.create('node', ['scripts/dist/copilot-browser.js', '--action', 'send']) を起動し stdin に JSON を書き込み、stdout をパースして response を返す。(4) エラー時は errorCode を含む CopilotBrowserError をスロー。(5) cdpPort は設定ストアから取得（デフォルト 9222）。
tasks.testStrategy: pnpm typecheck でエラーがないこと。Tauri dev 環境で sendToCopilot('test') を呼び出し、Node.js スクリプトが起動してレスポンスが返ることを確認。

tasks.title: UI 統合 — ステップ2に「Copilotに自動送信」ボタンを追加
tasks.description: +page.svelte のステップ2に「Copilotに自動送信」ボタンを追加する。押下時に sendToCopilot() を呼び出し、スピナー表示 → 完了後にテキストエリアを自動入力する。エラー時はインラインエラーを表示し手動入力にフォールバック可能とする。
tasks.details: 実装内容: (1) ステップ2の「依頼をコピー」ボタンの隣に「Copilotに自動送信 ▶」ボタンを追加。(2) 状態変数: isSendingToCopilot: boolean（スピナー制御）、copilotAutoError: string | null（エラー表示）。(3) ボタン押下フロー: isSendingToCopilot = true → sendToCopilot(relayPrompt) → copilotResponse = result → isSendingToCopilot = false。(4) エラー時: copilotAutoError にメッセージをセット → ステップ2内にインライン表示。テキストエリアは空のまま手動入力可能。(5) 自動入力後は通常の手動入力と同じパスで「確認する」ボタンに渡る。(6) 既存の「依頼をコピー」ボタンは残す（フォールバック用）。(7) Edge 未起動エラー（CDP_UNAVAILABLE）時は Edge 起動手順を案内するメッセージを表示。
tasks.testStrategy: svelte-check 0 errors、vite build 成功。Edge 未起動の状態でボタンを押して案内メッセージが表示されることを確認。正常時に Copilot レスポンスがテキストエリアに自動入力されることを手動確認。

tasks.title: エラーハンドリング強化 — エラーコード別の日本語メッセージ
tasks.description: CDP_UNAVAILABLE / NOT_LOGGED_IN / RESPONSE_TIMEOUT / COPILOT_ERROR の各エラーコードに対する日本語メッセージと UI での案内を整備する。すべてのエラーで手動入力フォールバックが可能であることを示す。
tasks.details: エラーコードと日本語メッセージ・対処案内の対応表: (1) CDP_UNAVAILABLE → 「Edge が CDP モードで起動していません。設定の「起動コマンドをコピー」ボタンで Edge を起動してから再試行してください。」(2) NOT_LOGGED_IN → 「M365 Copilot にログインしていません。Edge で M365 にログインしてから再試行してください。」(3) RESPONSE_TIMEOUT → 「Copilot の応答待機がタイムアウトしました。手動でコピー＆ペーストしてください。」(4) COPILOT_ERROR → 「Copilot がエラーを返しました: {message}。手動でコピー＆ペーストしてください。」(5) 全エラーにおいて「手動入力に切り替え」リンクを表示してテキストエリアにフォーカスする。lib/copilot-browser.ts に CopilotBrowserErrorCode enum を定義し、UI でマッピングテーブルを参照する実装とする。
tasks.testStrategy: 各エラーコードをシミュレート（CDP ポートを不正値に設定する等）してエラーメッセージが日本語で表示されることを確認。「手動入力に切り替え」リンクでテキストエリアにフォーカスが当たることを確認。

tasks.description: 実際の M365 Copilot 環境でブラウザ自動化の E2E フローを手動検証する。CSV ファイルを対象に Relay Agent の全3ステップを自動送信ボタン経由で完走させ、save-copy が正しく生成されることを確認する。
tasks.details: 検証シナリオ: (1) Edge CDP モード起動確認: 設定モーダルの「起動コマンドをコピー」でコマンドを取得し Edge を起動。CDP 接続が成功することを確認。(2) 正常フロー: 「Copilotに自動送信」ボタン押下から Copilot レスポンスのテキストエリア自動入力まで、手動操作なしで完了する。(3) レスポンス品質: auto-fix パイプラインを通した後のレスポンスが valid JSON として parse でき、スキーマバリデーションを通過する。(4) 保存: examples/revenue-workflow-demo.csv を対象に filter_rows + save_copy のプランを Copilot に生成させ、save-copy が正しく出力されることを確認。(5) エラーケース: CDP 未起動・未ログインの状態でそれぞれ適切なエラーメッセージが表示されることを確認。
